root.description: Whether to create only Route53 records (e.g. using separate AWS provider)
root.name: create_route53_records_only

root.description: Whether to allow overwrite of Route53 records
root.name: validation_allow_overwrite_records

root.description: Specifies whether certificate details should be added to a certificate transparency log
root.name: certificate_transparency_logging_preference

root.description: A domain name for which the certificate should be issued
root.name: domain_name

root.description: The TTL of DNS recursive resolvers to cache information about this record.
root.name: dns_ttl

root.description: A list of domain_validation_options created by the ACM certificate to create required Route53 records from it (used when create_route53_records_only is set to true)
root.name: acm_certificate_domain_validation_options

root.description: List of distinct domains and SANs (used when create_route53_records_only is set to true)
root.name: distinct_domain_names

root.description: Whether the certificate can be exported. Valid values are ENABLED or DISABLED (default). Note Issuing an exportable certificate is subject to additional charges
root.name: export

root.description: Whether to create ACM certificate
root.name: create_certificate

root.description: Define maximum timeout to wait for the validation to complete
root.name: validation_timeout

root.description: Which method to use for validation. DNS or EMAIL are valid. This parameter must not be set for certificates that were imported into ACM and then into Terraform.
root.name: validation_method

root.description: When validation is set to DNS, define whether to create the DNS records internally via Route53 or externally using any DNS provider
root.name: create_route53_records

root.description: When validation is set to DNS and the DNS validation records are set externally, provide the fqdns for the validation
root.name: validation_record_fqdns

root.description: Private Certificate Authority ARN for issuing private certificates
root.name: private_authority_arn

root.description: Whether to wait for the validation to complete
root.name: wait_for_validation

root.description: Region to create the resources into
root.name: region

root.description: The ID of the hosted zone to contain this record. Required when validating via Route53
root.name: zone_id

root.description: Map containing the Route53 Zone IDs for additional domains.
root.name: zones

root.description: Specifies the algorithm of the public and private key pair that your Amazon issued certificate uses to encrypt data
root.name: key_algorithm

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: Whether to validate certificate by creating Route53 record
root.name: validate_certificate

root.description: A list of domains that should be SANs in the issued certificate
root.name: subject_alternative_names

root.description: The domain name that you want ACM to use to send you validation emails. This domain name is the suffix of the email addresses that you want ACM to use.
root.name: validation_option

root.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource
root.name: tags

root.description: List of distinct domains names used for the validation.
root.name: distinct_domain_names

root.description: List of distinct domain validation options. This is useful if subject alternative names contain wildcards.
root.name: validation_domains

root.description: The ARN of the certificate
root.name: acm_certificate_arn

root.description: A list of attributes to feed into other resources to complete certificate validation. Can have more than one element, e.g. if SANs are defined. Only set if DNS-validation was used.
root.name: acm_certificate_domain_validation_options

root.description: Status of the certificate.
root.name: acm_certificate_status

root.description: A list of addresses that received a validation E-Mail. Only set if EMAIL-validation was used.
root.name: acm_certificate_validation_emails

root.description: List of FQDNs built using the zone domain and name.
root.name: validation_route53_record_fqdns

root::acm_certificate_domain_validation_.note: no example and no observed field names found in the module source; likely pass-through/context plumbing or a runtime-polymorphic value - stays honest `any`, see grep_module_docs for the raw module source
